rulings.Case Title: 5810-5816-5818 MISSION STREET OWNERS' ASSOCIATION, VS. ANGELA D WARREN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross Defendant 5810 5816 5818 Mission Street Owners Associations Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Action Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 583.310 Et Seq Failure To Bring To Trial Within Five Years And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 14, 2016, Line 2. CROSS DEFENDANT 5810-5816-5818 MISSION STREET OWNERS' ASSOCIATION, Cross Defendant 5810 5816 5818 Mission Street Owners Associations Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Action Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 583.310 Et Seq Failure To Bring To Trial Within Five Years And Request For Sanctions is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. On August 31, 2016 a dismissal with of all claims alleged in the Cross-Complaint was filed. The purported "exceptions" to the dismissal of cross-complaint have no effect on the dismissal as the cross-complaint does not contain any allegations regarding enforceable mediation agreements or bankruptcy fees. Therefore, the cross-complaint as alleged is dismissed with prejudice and the motion to dismiss is moot. Motion for sanctions per CCP 128.5 is GRANTED in the amount of $2,550. The 9 ½ months delay in filing a dismissal per the terms of the stipulation and Notice of Settlement is frivolous, harassing and caused unnecessary delay. No declaration is submitted in support of opposition to show otherwise. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ROSA MARIA QUINTANILLA VS. GRACIELA MUNOZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 14, 2016, Line 10. DEFENDANT GRACIELA MUNOZ, AURELIO MARTINEZ DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. Notice is fatally defective as it fails to comply with CC 1946.1. Compliance with substantive provisions of the Rent Ordinance is not a substitution for compliance with the procedural mandates of State Law. =(501/REQ)
